reason: The redesign changed isolated pages instead of migrating the application's entire visual language. The website currently contains multiple competing design systems, causing severe inconsistency in branding, user perception, navigation flow, and product trust.

objective.primary_goal: Transform QUORIN into a world-class premium ecommerce experience by replacing the entire visual language instead of recoloring individual pages.

current_state.home_page: Dark futuristic interface with neon accents, glowing particles, cyberpunk styling and gaming aesthetics.
current_state.xp_page: Soft cream minimal page with premium luxury styling.
current_state.product_page: Still using futuristic cards with red badges, cyan labels, glowing borders and dark gaming UI.
current_state.cart: Uses old futuristic sidebar with outdated spacing, typography and styling.
current_state.theme: Only isolated components changed while global design language remained untouched.

impact.user_experience: Feels like multiple websites stitched together.

critical_problem_analysis.diagnosis: The redesign incorrectly treated pages as independent projects. A premium ecommerce application is built from one unified design system where every page inherits identical visual rules. Currently, each route follows a different design philosophy, destroying immersion and reducing perceived quality.

mandatory_design_system_rebuild.description: Before modifying any additional page, the application must establish a single source of truth for every visual component.

accent_usage.gold: Used only for premium highlights, progress bars, active pricing, luxury badges and CTA emphasis.
accent_usage.terracotta: Used only for small highlights and brand warmth.

page_consistency_requirements.rule: Every page must appear as if designed by the same designer on the same day.

rules: Motion should communicate quality rather than excitement.

acceptance_criteria: There must be only ONE visual language across the application.
No page may retain cyberpunk styling.
Every component must inherit the global design tokens.
Changing the theme should update the entire application consistently.
All pages must feel like one premium luxury ecommerce platform.
The redesign should improve perceived product value before users even read product descriptions.
A user should never feel they navigated into another website.

completed_when: Every route shares identical design language.
Every component uses the new token system.
No legacy dark gaming UI remains.
Navigation, hero, product cards, cart, checkout, profile, XP and footer all look like one coherent premium product.
The application visually communicates luxury craftsmanship instead of futuristic gaming.
rejected_if: Different pages use different visual philosophies.
